decisions.reasoning: Warsh FOMC regime change = end of easy money. Statement rewritten, cutting bias removed, dot plot signals potential 2026 rate hike. Bond bear market just started. T10Y2Y spread flattened from 0.38 to 0.27 — market pricing rate hikes without growth confidence (stagflation setup). Kalshi sees >50% odds of hike this year. Short duration / long TBT is the trade.

Oil crashed from $82 to $75 on Iran peace deal. Near-term bearish from Hormuz reopening. But SPR at 41-year low (1983 levels!), US crude inventories drawn 52M barrels in 9 weeks. Structural underinvestment in new supply thesis intact. BofA says buy energy giants, Morgan Stanley sees upside in dividend-paying energy. Two-sided: oil could stay low if Iran supply floods market, or rebound sharply if Middle East tensions reignite or demand surprises.

Gold from $4,360 to $4,138 this week (-5.1%). Goldman slashed target by $500. DXY breaking out (100.78 from 99.5) = strong dollar headwind. Warsh hawkish = no rate cuts = real yields stay elevated. Iran deal removed safe-haven bid. Until Fed pivot signal emerges or dollar weakens, gold has no catalyst to rally. Central bank buying provides floor but near-term downside dominates.

AI storage new star. Gross margin crossed 50% structurally — not one-time. +16.10% Monday was S&P 500 biggest gainer. Sandisk spin-off value unlock potential. Bull case: AI data demand for storage is structural. Bear case: Momentum has been extreme — single-day 16% rallies invite profit-taking. Momentum-friendly but need entry discipline.

Apple chip deal catalyst is the turnaround signal the market needed. 18A-P production of most-advanced node is real. +10.65% Thursday with massive volume. Stock at $133.99 still far from analyst targets ($299 on NVDA for comparison). If Apple deal materializes, INTC foundry narrative goes from "struggling" to "credible alternative to TSMC".

Memory super-cycle intact. HBM3E exclusive supplier to Nvidia + DDR5 pricing power. +8.70% on Thursday confirmed momentum. Apple CEO called RAM costs "unsustainable" — confirms memory shortage is real and structural. Forward PE 9.9x is cheap for this cycle. TD Cowen sees more room after +224% YTD.

AI rotation back into semis confirmed this week. Apple price hike due to AI chip costs confirms sustained demand. Forward PE 16.5x for >50% earnings growth — valuation still reasonable. $25B bond issuance signals capex super-cycle, not dilution concern. Pullbacks on macro weakness (Warsh hawkishness) are buying opportunities.
